content.text: I now have a clear picture, and two of the three features have scope mismatches against the ROADMAP wording. Let me consult the advisor before writing anything.

Your scope analysis is correct, and I verified both mismatches against what you found — neither is constructed:

- **Feature 3:** `biplot_pq` really is a two-sample taxa comparison, `pcoa_pq` really doesn't exist, `plot_ordination_pq` really is the ordination wrapper.
- **Feature 2:** `vote_algorithm` + `resolve_vector_ranks` really do already live in `assign_vsearch_lca`.

But feature 2 is **stronger than "ambiguous" — it looks already-implemented**, and that's what should drive your developer question. Two concrete facts:

1. `assign_sintax`'s `min_bootstrap` (default 0.5) **is** "majority-vote at a configurable threshold." vsearch sintax's bootstrap value is the fraction of bootstrap iterations supporting each rank — a per-rank vote. Setting below-0.5 ranks to NA = majority vote. The face-value feature already exists (lines 1326–1329).
2. The multi-hit voting (consensus/rel_majority/abs_majority/unanimity) already exists for the LCA path. sintax returns one bootstrapped path per query, so there's **no set of competing hits to vote across** — the LCA-style vote doesn't map onto sintax output at all.

So don't ask "what did you mean" vaguely. Put the specific gap to the developer: *"`min_bootstrap` already gives majority-vote-at-threshold for sintax, and `vote_algorithm` already covers the LCA path — what's the missing piece?"* And offer the swap in the same question: **indicspecies extras** (your option 4, already vetted) is the clean replacement if feature 2 collapses.

**This blocks feature 2 only.** Don't implement it until the developer answers.

**Feature 3 does NOT block.** `plot_ordination_pq` is the only real ordination wrapper, so the target is forced — default to adding the envfit overlay there. Just confirm it in passing in the same question; don't gate work on it. (It builds the ordination via `phyloseq::ordinate`, so you'll pull scores + `sample_data` to feed `vegan::envfit` — straightforward.)

**Feature 1 is clean — start it now** while the developer answers 2 & 3; it's independent. One testability gotcha: `vsearch_clustering` has no `return_cmd` path (unlike `assign_sintax`/`assign_vsearch_lca`), so you can't assert on the built command string without vsearch installed. Follow the existing vsearch test pattern (`skip_if_not_installed` / `is_vsearch_installed`); the test will be skip-heavy on CI/CRAN, which matches the other vsearch tests. Don't refactor to expose the command just for testability.

You have what you need for the developer question — no need to round-trip back to me before asking.
The advisor confirms my findings and sharpens feature 2: it looks **already shipped**, not just ambiguous. Let me put the specific gap to you.

questions.question: Feature 2 (assign_sintax vote consensus) appears already implemented: `assign_sintax`'s `min_bootstrap` (default 0.5) already does majority-vote-at-threshold (sintax's bootstrap = fraction of iterations supporting each rank, set to NA below threshold), AND the multi-hit voting (consensus/rel_majority/abs_majority/unanimity via `vote_algorithm` + `resolve_vector_ranks`) already exists on the LCA path. sintax returns one bootstrapped path per query, so there's no set of competing hits to vote across. What's the missing piece?

options.label: Swap to indicspecies extras (Recommended)
options.description: Feature 2 looks already shipped. Build the vetted Low/easy ROADMAP option 4 instead: wrap indicspecies association/indicator functions (indval, phi, A, B) as a new pq-style function.

options.label: Build feature 2 anyway
options.description: There IS a missing piece I'll describe (e.g. a specific consensus output sintax lacks). Pick this and tell me exactly what's missing in the notes.

options.label: Drop it, ship only 2 features

questions.question: Feature 3 (envfit overlay): the ROADMAP names `biplot_pq()`/`pcoa_pq()`, but `biplot_pq()` is a two-sample taxa comparison (not an ordination) and `pcoa_pq()` doesn't exist. The only real ordination wrapper is `plot_ordination_pq()`. Where should the vegan::envfit() overlay go?

options.label: plot_ordination_pq() (Recommended)
options.description: Add the environmental-vector overlay to plot_ordination_pq(), the actual NMDS/PCoA wrapper. New args like add_envfit, envfit_fact.

options.label: New pcoa_pq() function
options.description: Create a dedicated pcoa_pq() ordination wrapper with built-in envfit, as the ROADMAP literally names. Larger scope.

content.content: Your questions have been answered: "Feature 2 (assign_sintax vote consensus) appears already implemented: `assign_sintax`'s `min_bootstrap` (default 0.5) already does majority-vote-at-threshold (sintax's bootstrap = fraction of iterations supporting each rank, set to NA below threshold), AND the multi-hit voting (consensus/rel_majority/abs_majority/unanimity via `vote_algorithm` + `resolve_vector_ranks`) already exists on the LCA path. sintax returns one bootstrapped path per query, so there's no set of competing hits to vote across. What's the missing piece?"="Swap to indicspecies extras (Recommended)", "Feature 3 (envfit overlay): the ROADMAP names `biplot_pq()`/`pcoa_pq()`, but `biplot_pq()` is a two-sample taxa comparison (not an ordination) and `pcoa_pq()` doesn't exist. The only real ordination wrapper is `plot_ordination_pq()`. Where should the vegan::envfit() overlay go?"="plot_ordination_pq() (Recommended)". You can now continue with these answers in mind.

questions.question: Feature 2 (assign_sintax vote consensus) appears already implemented: `assign_sintax`'s `min_bootstrap` (default 0.5) already does majority-vote-at-threshold (sintax's bootstrap = fraction of iterations supporting each rank, set to NA below threshold), AND the multi-hit voting (consensus/rel_majority/abs_majority/unanimity via `vote_algorithm` + `resolve_vector_ranks`) already exists on the LCA path. sintax returns one bootstrapped path per query, so there's no set of competing hits to vote across. What's the missing piece?

options.label: Swap to indicspecies extras (Recommended)
options.description: Feature 2 looks already shipped. Build the vetted Low/easy ROADMAP option 4 instead: wrap indicspecies association/indicator functions (indval, phi, A, B) as a new pq-style function.

options.label: Build feature 2 anyway
options.description: There IS a missing piece I'll describe (e.g. a specific consensus output sintax lacks). Pick this and tell me exactly what's missing in the notes.

options.label: Drop it, ship only 2 features
options.description: Just build feature 1 (cov args) and feature 3 (envfit). No third feature.

questions.question: Feature 3 (envfit overlay): the ROADMAP names `biplot_pq()`/`pcoa_pq()`, but `biplot_pq()` is a two-sample taxa comparison (not an ordination) and `pcoa_pq()` doesn't exist. The only real ordination wrapper is `plot_ordination_pq()`. Where should the vegan::envfit() overlay go?

options.label: plot_ordination_pq() (Recommended)
options.description: Add the environmental-vector overlay to plot_ordination_pq(), the actual NMDS/PCoA wrapper. New args like add_envfit, envfit_fact.

options.label: New pcoa_pq() function
options.description: Create a dedicated pcoa_pq() ordination wrapper with built-in envfit, as the ROADMAP literally names. Larger scope.

content.text: Feature 1 — adding `query_cov`/`target_cov` to `vsearch_clustering()`. First the roxygen param:

input.old_string:
#' @param vsearch_args (default : "--strand both") a one length character element defining other parameters to
#'   passed on to vsearch.
#' @param keep_temporary_files (logical, default: FALSE) Do we keep temporary files ?
#'   - temp.fasta (refseq in fasta or dna_seq sequences)
#'   - cluster.fasta (centroid if method = "vsearch")
#'   - temp.uc (clusters if method = "vsearch")
#'
#' @seealso [postcluster_pq()], [swarm_clustering()]
input.new_string:
#' @param vsearch_args (default : "--strand both") a one length character element defining other parameters to
#'   passed on to vsearch.
#' @param query_cov (default: NULL) Reject the alignment if the fraction of the
#'   query sequence aligned to the target is lower than this value. When NULL
#'   (default), the vsearch `--query_cov` option is not added to the command.
#'   See the `--query_cov` option in the
#'   [vsearch manual](https://github.com/torognes/vsearch).
#' @param target_cov (default: NULL) Reject the alignment if the fraction of the
#'   target sequence aligned to the query is lower than this value. When NULL
#'   (default), the vsearch `--target_cov` option is not added to the command.
#'   See the `--target_cov` option in the
#'   [vsearch manual](https://github.com/torognes/vsearch).
#' @param keep_temporary_files (logical, default: FALSE) Do we keep temporary files ?
#'   - temp.fasta (refseq in fasta or dna_seq sequences)
#'   - cluster.fasta (centroid if method = "vsearch")
#'   - temp.uc (clusters if method = "vsearch")
#'
#' @seealso [postcluster_pq()], [swarm_clustering()]

content.text: Now the formals and command build:

input.old_string:
vsearch_cluster_method = "--cluster_size",
  vsearch_args = "--strand both",
  keep_temporary_files = FALSE
) {
  dna <- physeq_or_string_to_dna(physeq = physeq, dna_seq = dna_seq)

  Biostrings::writeXStringSet(dna, paste0(tempdir(), "/", "temp.fasta"))

  system2(
    vsearchpath,
    paste0(
      paste0(
        " ",
        vsearch_cluster_method,
        " ",
        shQuote(paste0(tempdir(), "/", "temp.fasta")),
        " ",
        vsearch_args
      ),
      " -id ",
      id,
      " --centroids ",
      shQuote(paste0(tempdir(), "/", "cluster.fasta")),
      " --uc ",
      shQuote(paste0(tempdir(), "/", "temp.uc"))
    ),
    stdout = TRUE,
    stderr = TRUE
  )
input.new_string:
vsearch_cluster_method = "--cluster_size",
  vsearch_args = "--strand both",
  query_cov = NULL,
  target_cov = NULL,
  keep_temporary_files = FALSE
) {
  dna <- physeq_or_string_to_dna(physeq = physeq, dna_seq = dna_seq)

  Biostrings::writeXStringSet(dna, paste0(tempdir(), "/", "temp.fasta"))

  cov_args <- ""
  if (!is.null(query_cov)) {
    cov_args <- paste0(cov_args, " --query_cov ", query_cov)
  }
  if (!is.null(target_cov)) {
    cov_args <- paste0(cov_args, " --target_cov ", target_cov)
  }

  system2(
    vsearchpath,
    paste0(
      paste0(
        " ",
        vsearch_cluster_method,
        " ",
        shQuote(paste0(tempdir(), "/", "temp.fasta")),
        " ",
        vsearch_args
      ),
      " -id ",
      id,
      cov_args,
      " --centroids ",
      shQuote(paste0(tempdir(), "/", "cluster.fasta")),
      " --uc ",
      shQuote(paste0(tempdir(), "/", "temp.uc"))
    ),
    stdout = TRUE,
    stderr = TRUE
  )

file.content:
skip_on_cran()
sequences_ex <- c(
  "",
  "",
  ""
)

data("data_fungi", package = "MiscMetabar")
data("data_fungi_sp_known", package = "MiscMetabar")
data("data_fungi_mini", package = "MiscMetabar")
df_basidio <- subset_taxa(data_fungi, Phylum == "Basidiomycota")
df_basidio <-
  subset_taxa_pq(df_basidio, colSums(df_basidio@otu_table) > 1000)
# path_db <- "inst/extdata/100_sp_UNITE_sh_general_release_dynamic.fasta"

if (!MiscMetabar:::is_vsearch_installed()) {
  message(
    "vs_search_global() and asv2otu(..., method=vsearch) can't be tested when vsearch is not installed"
  )
} else {
  test_that("asv2otu works fine with vsearch method", {
    expect_s4_class(
      d_vs <-
        asv2otu(data_fungi_sp_known, method = "vsearch"),
      "phyloseq"
    )
    expect_s4_class(
      d_fast <- asv2otu(
        data_fungi_sp_known,
        method = "vsearch",
        vsearch_cluster_method = "--cluster_fast"
      ),
      "phyloseq"
    )
    expect_s3_class(
      asv2otu(dna_seq = sequences_ex, method = "vsearch"),
      "data.frame"
    )
    expect_true(sum(!d_fast@refseq == d_vs@refseq) > 0)

Additional arguments passed on to [indicspecies::multipatt()] function #' #' @return A ggplot2 object #' @export #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' if (requireNamespace("indicspecies")) { #' multipatt_pq(subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Time)), #' fact = "Time", control = permute::how(nperm = 99) #' ) #' multipatt_pq(subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Time)), #' fact = "Time", #' max.order = 1, control = permute::how(nperm = 99) #' ) #' } #' } #' #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details #' This function is mainly a wrapper of the work of others. #' Please make a reference to `indicspecies::multipatt()` if you #' use this function. multipatt_pq <- function( physeq, fact, p_adjust_method = "BH", pval = 0.05, control = permute::how(nperm = 999), ... ) { if (nlevels(as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) < 2) { stop( "The factor '", fact, "' must have at least two levels for ", "multipatt_pq (indicator species analysis requires at least 2 groups)." ) } physeq <- taxa_as_columns(physeq) res <- indicspecies::multipatt( as.matrix(physeq@otu_table), physeq@sam_data[[fact]], control = control, ... ) res_df <- res$sign res_df$p.adj <- p.adjust(res_df$p.value, method = p_adjust_method) res_df$taxon_names <- rownames(res_df) res_df_signif <- res_df |> filter(p.adj < pval) |> tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("s.")) p <- ggplot( res_df_signif, aes( x = taxon_names, y = name, size = 2 * value, color = stat ) ) + geom_point() + theme( axis.text.x = element_text( angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1 ) ) return(p) } content.text: Signature confirmed. Writing the `strassoc_pq()` wrapper:

input.content: ################################################################################ #' Strength of species-group associations for a phyloseq object #' #' @description #' #' #' #' #' A wrapper for the [indicspecies::strassoc()] function in the case of a #' `physeq` object. It computes the strength of the association between each #' taxon and the groups defined by `fact`, using one of the association #' indices provided by the `indicspecies` package (e.g. `IndVal`, the phi #' coefficient `r`, the specificity component `A`, or the fidelity component #' `B`). It complements [multipatt_pq()] which tests the statistical #' significance of those associations. #' #' @inheritParams clean_pq #' @param fact (required) Name of the factor in `physeq@sam_data` defining the #' groups of samples. #' @param func (chr, default "IndVal.g") The association index to compute. See #' `?indicspecies::strassoc()` for the full list. Common values are: #' - `"IndVal"` / `"IndVal.g"`: Dufrêne-Legendre indicator value (the `.g` #' variant equalises group sizes), #' - `"r"` / `"r.g"`: the point-biserial / phi coefficient of association, #' - `"A"`: specificity (positive predictive value) component, #' - `"B"`: fidelity (sensitivity) component. #' @param nboot_ci (int, default NULL) Number of bootstrap replicates used to #' estimate confidence intervals. When NULL (default), no bootstrap is #' performed and a single estimate is returned per taxon and group. #' @param alpha_ci (float, default 0.05) Error level for the bootstrap #' confidence intervals (only used when `nboot_ci` is not NULL). #' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to [indicspecies::strassoc()]. #' #' @return #' - When `nboot_ci` is NULL: a `tibble` with one row per taxon, a #' `taxon` column and one column per group containing the association #' value. #' - When `nboot_ci` is set: the list returned by [indicspecies::strassoc()] #' with the `lowerCI`, `stat` and `upperCI` matrices. #' #' @export #' @seealso [multipatt_pq()], [indicspecies::strassoc()] #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details #' This function is mainly a wrapper of the work of others. #' Please make a reference to `indicspecies::strassoc()` if you #' use this function. #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' if (requireNamespace("indicspecies")) { #' strassoc_pq( #' subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height)), #' fact = "Height", #' func = "IndVal.g" #' ) #' } #' } #' \dontrun{ #' if (requireNamespace("indicspecies")) { #' strassoc_pq( #' subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height)), #' fact = "Height", #' func = "A" #' ) #' strassoc_pq( #' subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height)), #' fact = "Height", #' func = "r", #' nboot_ci = 99 #' ) #' } #' } strassoc_pq <- function( physeq, fact, func = "IndVal.g", nboot_ci = NULL, alpha_ci = 0.05, ... ) { if (!requireNamespace("indicspecies", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg indicspecies} is required for {.fn strassoc_pq}. Please install it." ) } verify_pq(physeq) if (nlevels(as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) < 2) { cli::cli_abort( "The factor {.val {fact}} must have at least two levels for {.fn strassoc_pq}." ) } physeq <- taxa_as_columns(physeq) res <- indicspecies::strassoc( as.matrix(physeq@otu_table), physeq@sam_data[[fact]], func = func, nboot.ci = nboot_ci, alpha.ci = alpha_ci, ... ) if (is.null(nboot_ci)) { res <- tibble::as_tibble( as.data.frame(res), rownames = "taxon" ) } return(res) } ################################################################################

toolUseResult.originalFile: ################################################################################ #' Strength of species-group associations for a phyloseq object #' #' @description #' #' #' #' #' A wrapper for the [indicspecies::strassoc()] function in the case of a #' `physeq` object. It computes the strength of the association between each #' taxon and the groups defined by `fact`, using one of the association #' indices provided by the `indicspecies` package (e.g. `IndVal`, the phi #' coefficient `r`, the specificity component `A`, or the fidelity component #' `B`). It complements [multipatt_pq()] which tests the statistical #' significance of those associations. #' #' @inheritParams clean_pq #' @param fact (required) Name of the factor in `physeq@sam_data` defining the #' groups of samples. #' @param func (chr, default "IndVal.g") The association index to compute. See #' `?indicspecies::strassoc()` for the full list. Common values are: #' - `"IndVal"` / `"IndVal.g"`: Dufrêne-Legendre indicator value (the `.g` #' variant equalises group sizes), #' - `"r"` / `"r.g"`: the point-biserial / phi coefficient of association, #' - `"A"`: specificity (positive predictive value) component, #' - `"B"`: fidelity (sensitivity) component. #' @param nboot_ci (int, default NULL) Number of bootstrap replicates used to #' estimate confidence intervals. When NULL (default), no bootstrap is #' performed and a single estimate is returned per taxon and group. #' @param alpha_ci (float, default 0.05) Error level for the bootstrap #' confidence intervals (only used when `nboot_ci` is not NULL). #' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to [indicspecies::strassoc()]. #' #' @return #' - When `nboot_ci` is NULL: a `tibble` with one row per taxon, a #' `taxon` column and one column per group containing the association #' value. #' - When `nboot_ci` is set: the list returned by [indicspecies::strassoc()] #' with the `lowerCI`, `stat` and `upperCI` matrices. #' #' @export #' @seealso [multipatt_pq()], [indicspecies::strassoc()] #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details #' This function is mainly a wrapper of the work of others. #' Please make a reference to `indicspecies::strassoc()` if you #' use this function. #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' if (requireNamespace("indicspecies")) { #' strassoc_pq( #' subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height)), #' fact = "Height", #' func = "IndVal.g" #' ) #' } #' } #' \dontrun{ #' if (requireNamespace("indicspecies")) { #' strassoc_pq( #' subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height)), #' fact = "Height", #' func = "A" #' ) #' strassoc_pq( #' subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height)), #' fact = "Height", #' func = "r", #' nboot_ci = 99 #' ) #' } #' } strassoc_pq <- function( physeq, fact, func = "IndVal.g", nboot_ci = NULL, alpha_ci = 0.05, ... ) { if (!requireNamespace("indicspecies", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg indicspecies} is required for {.fn strassoc_pq}. Please install it." ) } verify_pq(physeq) if (nlevels(as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) < 2) { cli::cli_abort( "The factor {.val {fact}} must have at least two levels for {.fn strassoc_pq}." ) } physeq <- taxa_as_columns(physeq) res <- indicspecies::strassoc( as.matrix(physeq@otu_table), physeq@sam_data[[fact]], func = func, nboot.ci = nboot_ci, alpha.ci = alpha_ci, ... ) if (is.null(nboot_ci)) { res <- tibble::as_tibble( as.data.frame(res), rownames = "taxon" ) } return(res) } ################################################################################

content.text: vegan (Suggests, already used unguarded here) and rlang (Imports) are available. Let me add the roxygen params and example:

input.old_string:
#' @param ordination_method (string, default "NMDS") The ordination method to use
#'   in phyloseq::ordinate(). See ?phyloseq::ordinate for more details.
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to phyloseq::plot_ordination()
#' @returns A ggplot2 object
#' @export
#' @author Adrien Taudière
#'
#' @examples
#' library(patchwork)
#' plot_ordination_pq(data_fungi_mini, method = "robust.aitchison", color = "Height") +
#'   plot_ordination_pq(data_fungi_mini, method = "bray", color = "Height")
plot_ordination_pq <- function(
  physeq,
  method = "robust.aitchison",
  ordination_method = "NMDS",
  ...
) {
  verify_pq(physeq)
  physeq <- taxa_as_columns(physeq)
  dist_mat <- vegan::vegdist(unclass(physeq@otu_table), method = method)
  attr(dist_mat, "Labels") <- sample_names(physeq)
  p <- plot_ordination(
    physeq,
    ordination = ordinate(
      physeq,
      method = ordination_method,
      distance = dist_mat
    ),
    ...
  ) +
    labs(
      title = paste(ordination_method, "ordination"),
      subtitle = paste("Using ", method, "distance")
    )
  return(p)
}
input.new_string:
#' @param ordination_method (string, default "NMDS") The ordination method to use
#'   in phyloseq::ordinate(). See ?phyloseq::ordinate for more details.
#' @param add_envfit (logical, default FALSE) If TRUE, overlay environmental
#'   vectors (and factor centroids) fitted to the ordination using
#'   [vegan::envfit()]. Continuous variables are shown as arrows and factor
#'   variables as labelled centroids.
#' @param envfit_fact (default NULL) A character vector of variable names from
#'   `physeq@sam_data` to fit onto the ordination. When NULL (default) and
#'   `add_envfit = TRUE`, every variable of `physeq@sam_data` is used.
#' @param envfit_pval (float, default 1) Only environmental variables with an
#'   [vegan::envfit()] p-value lower than or equal to this threshold are drawn.
#'   The default (1) draws all of them.
#' @param envfit_arrow_mult (float, default 1) A multiplier applied to the
#'   environmental arrow lengths, to scale them for readability.
#' @param envfit_text_size (float, default 3) Text size for the environmental
#'   variable labels.
#' @param envfit_col (default "black") Color of the environmental arrows,
#'   centroids and labels.
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to phyloseq::plot_ordination()
#' @returns A ggplot2 object
#' @export
#' @seealso [vegan::envfit()], [phyloseq::plot_ordination()]
#' @author Adrien Taudière
#'
#' @examples
#' library(patchwork)
#' plot_ordination_pq(data_fungi_mini, method = "robust.aitchison", color = "Height") +
#'   plot_ordination_pq(data_fungi_mini, method = "bray", color = "Height")
#' \donttest{
#' plot_ordination_pq(
#'   subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height)),
#'   method = "bray",
#'   color = "Height",
#'   add_envfit = TRUE,
#'   envfit_fact = "Height"
#' )
#' }
plot_ordination_pq <- function(
  physeq,
  method = "robust.aitchison",
  ordination_method = "NMDS",
  add_envfit = FALSE,
  envfit_fact = NULL,
  envfit_pval = 1,
  envfit_arrow_mult = 1,
  envfit_text_size = 3,
  envfit_col = "black",
  ...
) {
  verify_pq(physeq)
  physeq <- taxa_as_columns(physeq)
  dist_mat <- vegan::vegdist(unclass(physeq@otu_table), method = method)
  attr(dist_mat, "Labels") <- sample_names(physeq)
  p <- plot_ordination(
    physeq,
    ordination = ordinate(
      physeq,
      method = ordination_method,
      distance = dist_mat
    ),
    ...
  ) +
    labs(
      title = paste(ordination_method, "ordination"),
      subtitle = paste("Using ", method, "distance")
    )

  if (add_envfit) {
    p <- .add_envfit_layers(
      p,
      physeq,
      envfit_fact = envfit_fact,
      envfit_pval = envfit_pval,
      envfit_arrow_mult = envfit_arrow_mult,
      envfit_text_size = envfit_text_size,
      envfit_col = envfit_col
    )
  }

  return(p)
}

# Overlay vegan::envfit() vectors and factor centroids on a plot_ordination
# ggplot object (internal helper for plot_ordination_pq()).
.add_envfit_layers <- function(
  p,
  physeq,
  envfit_fact,
  envfit_pval,
  envfit_arrow_mult,
  envfit_text_size,
  envfit_col
) {
  x_var <- rlang::as_label(p$mapping$x)
  y_var <- rlang::as_label(p$mapping$y)
  pts <- as.matrix(p$data[, c(x_var, y_var)])

  if (is.null(envfit_fact)) {
    envfit_fact <- colnames(physeq@sam_data)
  }
  env <- as.data.frame(as(physeq@sam_data, "data.frame"))[,
    envfit_fact,
    drop = FALSE
  ]
  env <- env[match(rownames(pts), sample_names(physeq)), , drop = FALSE]

  fit <- vegan::envfit(pts, env, na.rm = TRUE)

  arrows_df <- NULL
  if (!is.null(fit$vectors)) {
    keep <- fit$vectors$pvals <= envfit_pval
    if (any(keep)) {
      arrows_df <- as.data.frame(
        vegan::scores(fit, display = "vectors")[keep, , drop = FALSE]
      )
      arrows_df[[x_var]] <- arrows_df[[x_var]] * envfit_arrow_mult
      arrows_df[[y_var]] <- arrows_df[[y_var]] * envfit_arrow_mult
      arrows_df$label <- rownames(arrows_df)
    }
  }

  cent_df <- NULL
  if (!is.null(fit$factors)) {
    cent_df <- as.data.frame(vegan::scores(fit, display = "factors"))
    cent_df$label <- rownames(cent_df)
  }

  if (!is.null(arrows_df)) {
    p <- p +
      geom_segment(
        data = arrows_df,
        aes(
          x = 0,
          y = 0,
          xend = .data[[x_var]],
          yend = .data[[y_var]]
        ),
        arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.02, "npc")),
        color = envfit_col,
        inherit.aes = FALSE
      ) +
      geom_text(
        data = arrows_df,
        aes(x = .data[[x_var]], y = .data[[y_var]], label = label),
        color = envfit_col,
        size = envfit_text_size,
        inherit.aes = FALSE
      )
  }

  if (!is.null(cent_df)) {
    p <- p +
      geom_point(
        data = cent_df,
        aes(x = .data[[x_var]], y = .data[[y_var]]),
        shape = 4,
        color = envfit_col,
        inherit.aes = FALSE
      ) +
      geom_text(
        data = cent_df,
        aes(x = .data[[x_var]], y = .data[[y_var]], label = label),
        color = envfit_col,
        size = envfit_text_size,
        fontface = "italic",
        inherit.aes = FALSE
      )
  }

  return(p)
}

#' @param ordination_method (string, default "NMDS") The ordination method to use
#'   in phyloseq::ordinate(). See ?phyloseq::ordinate for more details.
#' @param add_envfit (logical, default FALSE) If TRUE, overlay environmental
#'   vectors (and factor centroids) fitted to the ordination using
#'   [vegan::envfit()]. Continuous variables are shown as arrows and factor
#'   variables as labelled centroids.
#' @param envfit_fact (default NULL) A character vector of variable names from
#'   `physeq@sam_data` to fit onto the ordination. When NULL (default) and
#'   `add_envfit = TRUE`, every variable of `physeq@sam_data` is used.
#' @param envfit_pval (float, default 1) Only environmental variables with an
#'   [vegan::envfit()] p-value lower than or equal to this threshold are drawn.
#'   The default (1) draws all of them.
#' @param envfit_arrow_mult (float, default 1) A multiplier applied to the
#'   environmental arrow lengths, to scale them for readability.
#' @param envfit_text_size (float, default 3) Text size for the environmental
#'   variable labels.
#' @param envfit_col (default "black") Color of the environmental arrows,
#'   centroids and labels.
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to phyloseq::plot_ordination()
#' @returns A ggplot2 object
#' @export
#' @seealso [vegan::envfit()], [phyloseq::plot_ordination()]
#' @author Adrien Taudière

#' \donttest{
#' plot_ordination_pq(
#'   subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height)),
#'   method = "bray",
#'   color = "Height",
#'   add_envfit = TRUE,
#'   envfit_fact = "Height"
#' )
#' }
plot_ordination_pq <- function(
  physeq,
  method = "robust.aitchison",
  ordination_method = "NMDS",
  add_envfit = FALSE,
  envfit_fact = NULL,
  envfit_pval = 1,
  envfit_arrow_mult = 1,
  envfit_text_size = 3,
  envfit_col = "black",
  ...

  dist_mat <- vegan::vegdist(unclass(physeq@otu_table), method = method)
  attr(dist_mat, "Labels") <- sample_names(physeq)
  p <- plot_ordination(
    physeq,
    ordination = ordinate(
      physeq,
      method = ordination_method,
      distance = dist_mat
    ),
    ...
  ) +
    labs(
      title = paste(ordination_method, "ordination"),
      subtitle = paste("Using ", method, "distance")
    )

    p <- .add_envfit_layers(

      envfit_arrow_mult = envfit_arrow_mult,

    )
  }

  return(p)
}

input.content:
data("data_fungi_mini", package = "MiscMetabar")
df_env <- subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height) & !is.na(Time))

test_that("plot_ordination_pq returns a ggplot without envfit", {
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  p <- suppressMessages(plot_ordination_pq(df_env, method = "bray"))
  expect_s3_class(p, "ggplot")
})

test_that("add_envfit overlays vectors for a continuous variable", {
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  p0 <- suppressMessages(plot_ordination_pq(df_env, method = "bray"))
  p1 <- suppressMessages(plot_ordination_pq(
    df_env,
    method = "bray",
    add_envfit = TRUE,
    envfit_fact = "Time"
  ))
  expect_s3_class(p1, "ggplot")
  expect_gt(length(p1$layers), length(p0$layers))
})

test_that("add_envfit overlays centroids for a factor variable", {
  skip_if_not_installed("vegan")
  p1 <- suppressMessages(plot_ordination_pq(
    df_env,
    method = "bray",
    add_envfit = TRUE,
    envfit_fact = "Height"
  ))
  expect_s3_class(p1, "ggplot")
})

content.content:
45:    indicspecies,
81:    vegan,
=== NEWS head ===
# MiscMetabar 0.16.8.9000
* Rarefaction across the package is now performed by an internal R-version-robust reimplementation rather than `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`, whose `replace = FALSE` code path errors with `invalid 'length.out' value` under recent R-devel (phyloseq issue #1753). The reimplementation is bit-identical to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()` for the same `seed`, depth and `replace` value (and is more correct in the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read). This affects `rarefy_pq()`, `adonis_pq()`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_test_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_pq()`, `biplot_pq()`, `ggvenn_pq()`, `upset_pq()`, `ggaluv_pq()` and `ggscatt_pq()`.
* `clean_pq()` gains a `tax_replace_NA_string` argument (default `FALSE`) to replace the literal strings `"NA"`, `"NA NA"`, `"NA NA NA"` (whitespace-separated repetitions of `NA`, a common artifact of pasting taxonomic ranks) in the `tax_table` with true `<NA>` values.
* `fastp()` is a new wrapper for the fastp software performing adapter detection, quality control and preprocessing of paired-end or single-end FASTQ files, with a companion `is_fastp_installed()` tool-presence helper.
* `hill_bar_pq()` now sets the "Error bars / Tukey HSD" caption once for the whole figure (via `patchwork::plot_annotation()`) instead of repeating it on every `q` panel, fixing the overlapping captions seen with several values of `q`. Panels where Tukey HSD pairwise comparisons were not run (no global Kruskal-Wallis significance) are flagged with a `sig_symbol` (default `"∅"`, the empty set, new argument) appended to their subtitle and explained once in the shared caption.
* `is_blastn_installed()` and `is_multiqc_installed()` are new tool-presence helpers, following the existing `is_cutadapt_installed()` / `is_vsearch_installed()` pattern.
* `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` exports the OTU table, taxonomy and sample data of a phyloseq object to separate CSV (or TSV) files following the GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) template layout (OTU IDs in rows, sample IDs in columns), the plain-text counterpart of `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()`.
* `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` exports the OTU table, sample data, taxonomy and reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) submission, with an optional Darwin Core sample-term check.
* `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call alpha- and beta-diversity overview of a phyloseq object for one sample variable, adapting the panels to the variable type: a Hill-number scatter via `ggscatt_pq()` for a numeric variable (gradient-colored ordination and UMAP, Venn/UpSet skipped), or Hill-number bar plots via `hill_bar_pq()` plus a Venn diagram (or an UpSet plot above `venn_max` levels) for a factor. Panels are returned as a named list by default, or assembled into a `patchwork` figure with `one_plot = TRUE`.
* `plot_volcano_pq()` draws a volcano plot (log2 fold change versus -log10 adjusted p-value) from a differential abundance result table such as `DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()` or `aldex_pq()` output, coloring taxa as up, down or not differentially abundant. The `fc` and `padj` arguments now default to `NULL` and are auto-detected from the input type: the raw list from `ancombc_pq()` is unwrapped to `$res` and its `lfc_*`/`q_*` columns are detected automatically (intercept excluded); `aldex_pq()` output is detected via `effect`/`wi.eBH` columns; `DESeq2::results()` is detected via `log2FoldChange`/`padj` columns.
* `rarefy_pq()` gains a `replace` argument (default `FALSE`, sampling without replacement) and accepts `seed = FALSE` to leave the random number generator untouched, mirroring `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`.
* `reshape_ggplot()` auto-wraps the title, subtitle, caption and axis labels of a ggplot2 object using `stringr::str_wrap()`, useful before combining several plots with `patchwork`. It gains dedicated `width_caption` and `fontsize_caption` arguments to control the caption wrapping width and font size.
}
[641 more lines]

8:* `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` exports the OTU table, sample data, taxonomy and reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) submission, with an optional Darwin Core sample-term check.
9:* `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call alpha- and beta-diversity overview of a phyloseq object for one sample variable, adapting the panels to the variable type: a Hill-number scatter via `ggscatt_pq()` for a numeric variable (gradient-colored ordination and UMAP, Venn/UpSet skipped), or Hill-number bar plots via `hill_bar_pq()` plus a Venn diagram (or an UpSet plot above `venn_max` levels) for a factor. Panels are returned as a named list by default, or assembled into a `patchwork` figure with `one_plot = TRUE`.
10:* `plot_volcano_pq()` draws a volcano plot (log2 fold change versus -log10 adjusted p-value) from a differential abundance result table such as `DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()` or `aldex_pq()` output, coloring taxa as up, down or not differentially abundant. 13:* `tax_bar_pq()` gains an `order_modality` argument (default `NULL`) to set the order of the `fact` modalities (the order of the bars); listing only a subset of modalities keeps just those bars (with an informative message) and an unknown value triggers an error listing the offending values.
14:* `tax_bar_pq()` gains a `ribbon_hide_zero` argument (default `TRUE`) that, when `add_ribbon = TRUE`, suppresses the ribbon of a taxon between two adjacent bars whenever its value is zero in either connected bar, avoiding ribbons that collapse to a flat line.

# MiscMetabar 0.16.8.9000
* Rarefaction across the package is now performed by an internal R-version-robust reimplementation rather than `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`, whose `replace = FALSE` code path errors with `invalid 'length.out' value` under recent R-devel (phyloseq issue #1753). The reimplementation is bit-identical to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()` for the same `seed`, depth and `replace` value (and is more correct in the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read). This affects `rarefy_pq()`, `adonis_pq()`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_test_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_pq()`, `biplot_pq()`, `ggvenn_pq()`, `upset_pq()`, `ggaluv_pq()` and `ggscatt_pq()`.
* `clean_pq()` gains a `tax_replace_NA_string` argument (default `FALSE`) to replace the literal strings `"NA"`, `"NA NA"`, `"NA NA NA"` (whitespace-separated repetitions of `NA`, a common artifact of pasting taxonomic ranks) in the `tax_table` with true `<NA>` values.
* `fastp()` is a new wrapper for the fastp software performing adapter detection, quality control and preprocessing of paired-end or single-end FASTQ files, with a companion `is_fastp_installed()` tool-presence helper.
* `hill_bar_pq()` now sets the "Error bars / Tukey HSD" caption once for the whole figure (via `patchwork::plot_annotation()`) instead of repeating it on every `q` panel, fixing the overlapping captions seen with several values of `q`. Panels where Tukey HSD pairwise comparisons were not run (no global Kruskal-Wallis significance) are flagged with a `sig_symbol` (default `"∅"`, the empty set, new argument) appended to their subtitle and explained once in the shared caption.
* `is_blastn_installed()` and `is_multiqc_installed()` are new tool-presence helpers, following the existing `is_cutadapt_installed()` / `is_vsearch_installed()` pattern.
* `phyloseq_to_MDT_csv()` exports the OTU table, taxonomy and sample data of a phyloseq object to separate CSV (or TSV) files following the GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) template layout (OTU IDs in rows, sample IDs in columns), the plain-text counterpart of `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()`.
* `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` exports the OTU table, sample data, taxonomy and reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT) submission, with an optional Darwin Core sample-term check.
* `plot_overview_pq()` produces a single-call alpha- and beta-diversity overview of a phyloseq object for one sample variable, adapting the panels to the variable type: a Hill-number scatter via `ggscatt_pq()` for a numeric variable (gradient-colored ordination and UMAP, Venn/UpSet skipped), or Hill-number bar plots via `hill_bar_pq()` plus a Venn diagram (or an UpSet plot above `venn_max` levels) for a factor. Panels are returned as a named list by default, or assembled into a `patchwork` figure with `one_plot = TRUE`.
* `plot_volcano_pq()` draws a volcano plot (log2 fold change versus -log10 adjusted p-value) from a differential abundance result table such as `DESeq2`, `ancombc_pq()` or `aldex_pq()` output, coloring taxa as up, down or not differentially abundant. The `fc` and `padj` arguments now default to `NULL` and are auto-detected from the input type: the raw list from `ancombc_pq()` is unwrapped to `$res` and its `lfc_*`/`q_*` columns are detected automatically (intercept excluded); `aldex_pq()` output is detected via `effect`/`wi.eBH` columns; `DESeq2::results()` is detected via `log2FoldChange`/`padj` columns.
* `rarefy_pq()` gains a `replace` argument (default `FALSE`, sampling without replacement) and accepts `seed = FALSE` to leave the random number generator untouched, mirroring `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`.
* `reshape_ggplot()` auto-wraps the title, subtitle, caption and axis labels of a ggplot2 object using `stringr::str_wrap()`, useful before combining several plots with `patchwork`. It gains dedicated `width_caption` and `fontsize_caption` arguments to control the caption wrapping width and font size.
* `tax_bar_pq()` gains an `order_modality` argument (default `NULL`) to set the order of the `fact` modalities (the order of the bars); listing only a subset of modalities keeps just those bars (with an informative message) and an unknown value triggers an error listing the offending values.
* `tax_bar_pq()` gains a `ribbon_hide_zero` argument (default `TRUE`) that, when `add_ribbon = TRUE`, suppresses the ribbon of a taxon between two adjacent bars whenever its value is zero in either connected bar, avoiding ribbons that collapse to a flat line.

# MiscMetabar 0.16.8

# MiscMetabar 0.16.8
input.new_string:
* `tax_bar_pq()` gains a `ribbon_hide_zero` argument (default `TRUE`) that, when `add_ribbon = TRUE`, suppresses the ribbon of a taxon between two adjacent bars whenever its value is zero in either connected bar, avoiding ribbons that collapse to a flat line.

content.subject: Switch to dev branch
content.description: After developer commits/stashes master changes, check out dev and confirm clean starting diff.

content.subject: Feature: vs_cluster_subseq cov args
content.description: Expose --query_cov and --target_cov as direct args in vs_cluster_subseq() (and postcluster_pq()). Write R + test_ file, validate.

content.subject: Feature: indicspecies wrapper
content.description: New pq-style function wrapping indicspecies association/indicator functions (indval, phi, A, B). New R/<slug>.R + test_<slug>.R, validate.

content.subject: Feature: envfit in ordination
content.description: Integrate vegan::envfit() into biplot_pq()/pcoa_pq() to overlay environmental vectors. R + test_ file, validate.

content.subject: Consolidate global files
content.description: One pass: NEWS.md, _pkgdown.yml, DESCRIPTION Suggests, WORDLIST, air format, document(), carryover check, spelling, full test suite.

content.subject: Quarto demo for all features
content.description: Write arround_MiscMetabar demo qmd covering all 3 features, verify chunks evaluate on 5-sample subset.

content.subject: ROADMAP cleanup & report
content.description: Delete shipped bullets from ROADMAP.md, report table, note carryover, leave uncommitted on dev.

toolUseResult.originalFile: ################################################################################ #' Strength of species-group associations for a phyloseq object #' #' @description #' #' #' #' #' A wrapper for the [indicspecies::strassoc()] function in the case of a #' `physeq` object. It computes the strength of the association between each #' taxon and the groups defined by `fact`, using one of the association #' indices provided by the `indicspecies` package (e.g. `IndVal`, the phi #' coefficient `r`, the specificity component `A`, or the fidelity component #' `B`). It complements [multipatt_pq()] which tests the statistical #' significance of those associations. #' #' @inheritParams clean_pq #' @param fact (required) Name of the factor in `physeq@sam_data` defining the #' groups of samples. #' @param func (chr, default "IndVal.g") The association index to compute. See #' `?indicspecies::strassoc()` for the full list. Common values are: #' - `"IndVal"` / `"IndVal.g"`: Dufrêne-Legendre indicator value (the `.g` #' variant equalises group sizes), #' - `"r"` / `"r.g"`: the point-biserial / phi coefficient of association, #' - `"A"`: specificity (positive predictive value) component, #' - `"B"`: fidelity (sensitivity) component. #' @param nboot_ci (int, default NULL) Number of bootstrap replicates used to #' estimate confidence intervals. When NULL (default), no bootstrap is #' performed and a single estimate is returned per taxon and group. #' @param alpha_ci (float, default 0.05) Error level for the bootstrap #' confidence intervals (only used when `nboot_ci` is not NULL). #' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to [indicspecies::strassoc()]. #' #' @return #' - When `nboot_ci` is NULL: a `tibble` with one row per taxon, a #' `taxon` column and one column per group containing the association #' value. #' - When `nboot_ci` is set: the list returned by [indicspecies::strassoc()] #' with the `lowerCI`, `stat` and `upperCI` matrices. #' #' @export #' @seealso [multipatt_pq()], [indicspecies::strassoc()] #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details #' This function is mainly a wrapper of the work of others. #' Please make a reference to `indicspecies::strassoc()` if you #' use this function. #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' if (requireNamespace("indicspecies")) { #' strassoc_pq( #' subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height)), #' fact = "Height", #' func = "IndVal.g" #' ) #' } #' } #' \dontrun{ #' if (requireNamespace("indicspecies")) { #' strassoc_pq( #' subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height)), #' fact = "Height", #' func = "A" #' ) #' strassoc_pq( #' subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height)), #' fact = "Height", #' func = "IndVal.g", #' nboot_ci = 99 #' ) #' } #' } strassoc_pq <- function( physeq, fact, func = "IndVal.g", nboot_ci = NULL, alpha_ci = 0.05, ... ) { if (!requireNamespace("indicspecies", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg indicspecies} is required for {.fn strassoc_pq}. Please install it." ) } verify_pq(physeq) if (nlevels(as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) < 2) { cli::cli_abort( "The factor {.val {fact}} must have at least two levels for {.fn strassoc_pq}." ) } physeq <- taxa_as_columns(physeq) res <- indicspecies::strassoc( as.matrix(physeq@otu_table), physeq@sam_data[[fact]], func = func, nboot.ci = nboot_ci, alpha.ci = alpha_ci, ... ) if (is.null(nboot_ci)) { res <- tibble::as_tibble( as.data.frame(res), rownames = "taxon" ) } return(res) } ################################################################################

---
title: "MiscMetabar — New features (dev 0.16.8.9000)"
author: "Adrien Taudière"
date: today

---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
# Load the development version of MiscMetabar from the package source.
# Adjust the path if your working directory differs.
devtools::load_all("~/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/pqverse/pqverse_pkg/MiscMetabar")
library(ggplot2)
```

This document explains and illustrates the features added to **MiscMetabar**
during the `0.16.8.9000` development cycle. They were implemented from the
*pqverse* `ROADMAP.md` (`MiscMetabar` section) and group into four themes:

| Theme | Function(s) | Roadmap tag |
|---|---|---|
| Data cleaning | `clean_pq(tax_replace_NA_string = )` | High / easy |
| Visualisation | `reshape_ggplot()` | High / easy |
| Data I/O | `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` | High / easy |
| Bioinfo wrappers | `fastp()`, `is_fastp_installed()` | High / easy |
| Tool detection | `is_blastn_installed()`, `is_multiqc_installed()` | Medium / easy |

All examples use the small bundled dataset `data_fungi_mini`.

---

# 1. `clean_pq()` — clean `"NA"` / `"NA NA"` strings

## The problem

When a taxonomy table is built by *pasting* taxonomic ranks together (e.g.
`paste(Genus, Species)`), missing ranks frequently leave literal string
artifacts such as `"NA"`, `"NA NA"` or `"NA NA NA"` inside the `tax_table`.
These are **character strings**, not the true `<NA>` missing value, so they
silently pollute grouping, counting and plotting.

`clean_pq()` already exposed `tax_replace_to_NA` (which targets NA-*like*
patterns: `unclassified`, `unknown`, single `NA`, ...). The new
`tax_replace_NA_string` argument specifically targets the multi-token

content.text: I have the conventions. Writing the demo, using 5-sample subsetting and guards so every chunk evaluates regardless of external tools:

input.content:
---
title: "MiscMetabar — ROADMAP batch (dev 0.16.8.9000)"
author: "Adrien Taudière"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: show
    df-print: paged
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
# Load the development version of MiscMetabar from the package source.
# Adjust the path if your working directory differs.
devtools::load_all("~/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/pqverse/pqverse_pkg/MiscMetabar")
library(ggplot2)
data("data_fungi_mini", package = "MiscMetabar")

# data_fungi_mini has 137 samples; cap per-sample work to 5 samples.
dfm5 <- prune_samples(sample_names(data_fungi_mini)[1:5], data_fungi_mini)
```

This document illustrates the three features implemented from the *pqverse*
`ROADMAP.md` (`MiscMetabar` section) during the `0.16.8.9000` development cycle.

| Theme | Function(s) | Roadmap tag |
|---|---|---|
| Data I/O | `vsearch_clustering()` / `postcluster_pq()` coverage args | Medium / easy |
| Analysis / Statistics | `strassoc_pq()` | Low / easy |
| Visualisation | `plot_ordination_pq(add_envfit = )` | Medium / easy |

---

# 1. `vsearch_clustering()` / `postcluster_pq()` — coverage thresholds

## What changed

VSEARCH's `--cluster_*` commands accept two alignment-coverage filters,
`--query_cov` and `--target_cov`, that reject an alignment unless a minimum
fraction of the query (resp. target) sequence is aligned. Previously these
could only be reached by hand-crafting the `vsearch_args` string. They are now
first-class arguments of both `vsearch_clustering()` and `postcluster_pq()`
(the latter only when `method = "vsearch"`), defaulting to `NULL` (flag not
added, i.e. unchanged behaviour).

```{r cov-formals}
# The new arguments are exposed directly:
names(formals(vsearch_clustering))[
  names(formals(vsearch_clustering)) %in% c("query_cov", "target_cov")
]
```

```{r cov-run}
# Requires the vsearch binary. The chunk evaluates either way.
if (is_vsearch_installed()) {
  d_cov <- vsearch_clustering(dfm5, query_cov = 0.9, target_cov = 0.9)
  cat("Taxa before:", ntaxa(dfm5), "- after clustering:", ntaxa(d_cov), "\n")
} else {
  message("vsearch not installed: skipping the live clustering example.")
}
```

---

# 2. `strassoc_pq()` — strength of species–group associations

## What it does

`multipatt_pq()` already wraps `indicspecies::multipatt()` to *test* indicator
species. `strassoc_pq()` complements it by *quantifying* the strength of the
association between each taxon and the groups of a sample variable, using any of
the `indicspecies::strassoc()` indices: the Dufrêne-Legendre indicator value
`IndVal` / `IndVal.g`, the point-biserial / phi coefficient `r`, the specificity
component `A` or the fidelity component `B`.

```{r strassoc-indval}
df_h <- subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height))
indval <- strassoc_pq(df_h, fact = "Height", func = "IndVal.g")
head(indval)
```

A single estimate per taxon and group is returned as a tidy tibble. Switching the
index is a one-argument change:

```{r strassoc-A}
specificity <- strassoc_pq(df_h, fact = "Height", func = "A")
head(specificity)
```

With `nboot_ci`, bootstrap confidence intervals are returned as a list of
`lowerCI` / `stat` / `upperCI` matrices:

```{r strassoc-ci}
ci <- strassoc_pq(df_h, fact = "Height", func = "IndVal.g", nboot_ci = 99)
names(ci)
head(ci$stat)
```

---

# 3. `plot_ordination_pq(add_envfit = TRUE)` — environmental overlays

## What changed

`plot_ordination_pq()` gains an `add_envfit` switch that fits sample variables
onto the ordination with `vegan::envfit()` and overlays them: continuous
variables become arrows, factor variables become labelled centroids. The
selection and styling are controlled by `envfit_fact`, `envfit_pval`,
`envfit_arrow_mult`, `envfit_text_size` and `envfit_col`.

```{r envfit-cont}
df_env <- subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height) & !is.na(Time))

# Continuous variable (Time) -> arrow. PCoA keeps the example quiet/deterministic.
plot_ordination_pq(
  df_env,
  method = "bray",
  ordination_method = "PCoA",
  color = "Height",
  add_envfit = TRUE,
  envfit_fact = "Time"
)
```

---
title: "MiscMetabar — ROADMAP batch (dev 0.16.8.9000)"
author: "Adrien Taudière"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: show
    df-print: paged
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
# Load the development version of MiscMetabar from the package source.
# Adjust the path if your working directory differs.
devtools::load_all("~/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/pqverse/pqverse_pkg/MiscMetabar")
library(ggplot2)
data("data_fungi_mini", package = "MiscMetabar")

# data_fungi_mini has 137 samples; cap per-sample work to 5 samples.
dfm5 <- prune_samples(sample_names(data_fungi_mini)[1:5], data_fungi_mini)
```

This document illustrates the three features implemented from the *pqverse*
`ROADMAP.md` (`MiscMetabar` section) during the `0.16.8.9000` development cycle.

| Theme | Function(s) | Roadmap tag |
|---|---|---|
| Data I/O | `vsearch_clustering()` / `postcluster_pq()` coverage args | Medium / easy |
| Analysis / Statistics | `strassoc_pq()` | Low / easy |
| Visualisation | `plot_ordination_pq(add_envfit = )` | Medium / easy |

---

# 1. `vsearch_clustering()` / `postcluster_pq()` — coverage thresholds

## What changed

VSEARCH's `--cluster_*` commands accept two alignment-coverage filters,
`--query_cov` and `--target_cov`, that reject an alignment unless a minimum
fraction of the query (resp. target) sequence is aligned. Previously these
could only be reached by hand-crafting the `vsearch_args` string. They are now
first-class arguments of both `vsearch_clustering()` and `postcluster_pq()`
(the latter only when `method = "vsearch"`), defaulting to `NULL` (flag not
added, i.e. unchanged behaviour).

```{r cov-formals}
# The new arguments are exposed directly:
names(formals(vsearch_clustering))[
  names(formals(vsearch_clustering)) %in% c("query_cov", "target_cov")
]
```

```{r cov-run}
# Requires the vsearch binary. The chunk evaluates either way.
if (is_vsearch_installed()) {
  d_cov <- vsearch_clustering(dfm5, query_cov = 0.9, target_cov = 0.9)
  cat("Taxa before:", ntaxa(dfm5), "- after clustering:", ntaxa(d_cov), "\n")
} else {
  message("vsearch not installed: skipping the live clustering example.")
}
```

---

# 2. `strassoc_pq()` — strength of species–group associations

## What it does

`multipatt_pq()` already wraps `indicspecies::multipatt()` to *test* indicator
species. `strassoc_pq()` complements it by *quantifying* the strength of the
association between each taxon and the groups of a sample variable, using any of
the `indicspecies::strassoc()` indices: the Dufrêne-Legendre indicator value
`IndVal` / `IndVal.g`, the point-biserial / phi coefficient `r`, the specificity
component `A` or the fidelity component `B`.

```{r strassoc-indval}
df_h <- subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height))
indval <- strassoc_pq(df_h, fact = "Height", func = "IndVal.g")
head(indval)
```

A single estimate per taxon and group is returned as a tidy tibble. Switching the
index is a one-argument change:

```{r strassoc-A}
specificity <- strassoc_pq(df_h, fact = "Height", func = "A")
head(specificity)
```

With `nboot_ci`, bootstrap confidence intervals are returned as a list of
`lowerCI` / `stat` / `upperCI` matrices:

```{r strassoc-ci}
ci <- strassoc_pq(df_h, fact = "Height", func = "IndVal.g", nboot_ci = 99)
names(ci)
head(ci$stat)
```

---

# 3. `plot_ordination_pq(add_envfit = TRUE)` — environmental overlays

## What changed

`plot_ordination_pq()` gains an `add_envfit` switch that fits sample variables
onto the ordination with `vegan::envfit()` and overlays them: continuous
variables become arrows, factor variables become labelled centroids. The
selection and styling are controlled by `envfit_fact`, `envfit_pval`,
`envfit_arrow_mult`, `envfit_text_size` and `envfit_col`.

```{r envfit-cont}
df_env <- subset_samples(data_fungi_mini, !is.na(Height) & !is.na(Time))

# Continuous variable (Time) -> arrow. PCoA keeps the example quiet/deterministic.
plot_ordination_pq(
  df_env,
  method = "bray",
  ordination_method = "PCoA",
  color = "Height",
  add_envfit = TRUE,
  envfit_fact = "Time"
)
```

```{r envfit-factor}
# Factor variable (Height) -> labelled centroids.
plot_ordination_pq(
  df_env,
  method = "bray",
  ordination_method = "PCoA",
  color = "Height",
  add_envfit = TRUE,
  envfit_fact = "Height"
)
```

---

```{r session}
sessionInfo()
```

content.text: All three ROADMAP bullets removed. Deliverables are durable (code, tests, NEWS, pkgdown, docs, demo all written and green). Let me consult the advisor before declaring done.

You're essentially done, and the validation is real — not just green-on-structure. The layer-count assertion in the Time test actually forces the arrow path to execute (geom_segment + geom_text), and the render exercised the factor-centroid path with `envfit_fact = "Height"`. The strassoc orientation check (`nrow == ntaxa`) would have failed if the matrix were transposed (ntaxa ≫ 2 groups). So the hard paths are covered, not just `expect_s3_class`. Good.

**One documented path your checks never exercised — verify before handoff:** the advertised default `envfit_fact = NULL` (→ "every variable of `physeq@sam_data` is used"). Every test and the demo passes an *explicit* `envfit_fact`, so this branch is untested. On `data_fungi_mini` it feeds `X`, `Sample_names`, `Tree_name`, `Sample_id` into `vegan::envfit` — ID/character columns with up to 137 unique levels.

Run one line: `plot_ordination_pq(df_env, method="bray", ordination_method="PCoA", add_envfit=TRUE)`.
- If it errors → the documented default is broken; either restrict the NULL case to numeric columns or make `envfit_fact` required, and amend the roxygen. **This blocks** (a feature whose default arg errors isn't shippable).
- If it just produces a cluttered overlay → not a blocker, but worth a sentence in the doc steering users to pass `envfit_fact`.

That's the only correctness gap I see.

**Report must-haves** (the developer is reviewing an uncommitted diff, so surface what isn't self-evident):

1. **The two carryover `.Rd` are independent of this batch.** `plot_volcano_pq.Rd` and `rarefy_pq.Rd` are doc-sync from prior commits that edited roxygen without re-running `document()`. Keeping them regenerated is correct (don't revert — that re-desyncs them), but tell the developer plainly so they can commit those separately from the feature batch if they want clean history. You already flagged this mid-run; just carry it into the final report.

2. **Restate where each feature landed vs. the ROADMAP names** — `vs_cluster_subseq` → `vsearch_clustering()`/`postcluster_pq()`; `biplot_pq`/`pcoa_pq` → `plot_ordination_pq()`. The developer approved these at decision time, so it's confirmation not surprise, but the report is the durable record.

3. **State R CMD check was not run, and offer `/r-check`** (skill Stage 5.2 requires this).

Then open the html (Stage 5.3) and you're finished. Don't re-open the implementation — it's sound; just close the `envfit_fact = NULL` question and write the report.
Good catch — the advisor flags the untested `envfit_fact = NULL` default. Let me exercise it:

content.text:
Done. Three ROADMAP features shipped, individually validated and consolidated in one pass. Full suite green (**FAIL 0 | PASS 1177**), demo rendered.

## Features shipped

| Feature | Function(s) | Test | NEWS | pkgdown | man |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Coverage args | `vsearch_clustering()`, `postcluster_pq()` gain `query_cov`/`target_cov` | `test_postcluster_cov.R` ✓ | ✓ | (existing) | ✓ |
| Indicator strength | **new** `strassoc_pq()` (wraps `indicspecies::strassoc()`) | `test_strassoc_pq.R` ✓ | ✓ | ✓ (Diff. abundance) | ✓ new |
| Ordination overlay | `plot_ordination_pq()` gains `add_envfit` + 5 styling args | `test_plot_ordination_envfit.R` ✓ | ✓ | (existing) | ✓ |

**Where each landed vs. the ROADMAP names** (you approved these at decision time — recording for the durable history):
- `vs_cluster_subseq()` (a Rsearch function) → implemented in MiscMetabar's `vsearch_clustering()`/`postcluster_pq()`, the "maybe add to postcluster_pq" part of the bullet.
- Feature 2 swapped from `assign_sintax` (already-shipped: `min_bootstrap` + the LCA `vote_algorithm` cover it) → `strassoc_pq()` (the `indicspecies` extras bullet).
- `biplot_pq`/`pcoa_pq` (a two-sample comparison / a non-existent function) → `plot_ordination_pq()`, the real ordination wrapper.

## Validation
- Each feature validated individually green before the next.
- One consolidation pass: NEWS (3 bullets, re-sorted alphabetically), `_pkgdown.yml` (`strassoc_pq`), `air format`, single `document()`, spelling (`biserial`, `Dufrêne`, `IndVal` added to WORDLIST; `equalises`→`equalizes`), full suite.
- `DESCRIPTION` unchanged — `indicspecies` and `vegan` were already in Suggests.
- Verified the documented `envfit_fact = NULL` default actually runs (returns a ggplot, doesn't error) — added a doc sentence steering users to pass an explicit subset to avoid clutter from ID columns.
- Demo `arround_MiscMetabar/roadmap_batch_demo.qmd` rendered to HTML; every chunk evaluated (opened in your browser).

## ⚠️ Carryover to review separately
`man/plot_volcano_pq.Rd` and `man/rarefy_pq.Rd` changed but are **not** from this batch — prior commits edited the roxygen source without re-running `document()`, so the committed `.Rd` had drifted. Keeping them regenerated is the correct state (don't revert — that re-desyncs them), but you may want to commit those two separately from the feature batch for clean history.

## Notes
- Everything is left **uncommitted on `dev`** for you to commit. ROADMAP.md and the demo live outside the package git repo (the pqverse workspace isn't a git repo), so those are plain filesystem edits.
- **R CMD check was not run.** Run `/r-check` when you're ready to validate styling + docs + check before release.

Base directory for this skill: /home/adrien/.claude/skills/r-feature-batch

Implement a batch of **3-4 ROADMAP features** for a pqverse R package, **one feature at a
time** in the `dev` working tree, then finish with **one consolidation pass** that does every
global edit exactly once and **one Quarto demo** illustrating all the new features together.

This skill reproduces the workflow proven in MiscMetabar 0.16.8.9000 (clean_pq NA-cleanup,
reshape_ggplot, phyloseq_to_MDT_excel, fastp, tool-presence checks).

## The discipline that keeps the diff clean

While implementing each feature, touch **only its own files** — `R/<slug>.R` and
`tests/testthat/test_<slug>.R`. Defer **every global file** to the single consolidation stage
at the end:

`NEWS.md`, `_pkgdown.yml`, `DESCRIPTION` (Suggests), `inst/WORDLIST`, `NAMESPACE`, `man/`.

Why batch them: NEWS bullets are alphabetised across the *whole* set at once; `document()`
and `air format` run once over the final tree (not N times); and a single `git diff
NAMESPACE man/` at the end cleanly reveals any carryover. Editing globals per-feature just
creates churn and re-sorting work.

## Arguments

`dbpq` may contain, in any order:

- A count (`3` or `4`) — how many features to implement. If absent, ask.
- `--filter "High/easy"` — priority/facility filter for candidate selection. Default:
  `High/easy` plus `Medium/easy`. Prefer items carrying a `(source: …)` prototype pointer.
- `--package <name>` — target package. If absent, read `Package:` from `DESCRIPTION` in the
  cwd. Resolves to `pqverse_pkg/<name>/` from the workspace root.

## Commits

**Leave everything uncommitted on `dev` for the developer.** The rule is "Don't commit, the
developer do." This skill never commits, branches, or merges.

---

## Stage 0 — Preconditions & context

1. Confirm cwd (or `--package` target) is an R package: `DESCRIPTION` present. Read `Package:`.
2. Locate the workspace root and `ROADMAP.md` (pqverse root, e.g.
   `/home/adrien/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/pqverse/ROADMAP.md`).
3. Confirm the current branch is `dev` (or ask). Run `git status` so you know the starting
   diff and can keep the new work scoped.
4. Identify the demo target dir: `<workspace>/arround_MiscMetabar/`.

## Stage 1 — Select features

1. Read the target package's section of `ROADMAP.md`. Extract bullets matching the filter.
   **Strongly prefer items with a `(source: …)` pointer** — the prototype already exists, so
   they're lower-risk and faster. Open each pointer (often
   `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd:<lines>`) to confirm scope.
2. Present the candidates to the user with `AskUserQuestion` (`multiSelect: true`). Let them
   pick the 3-4 to build. Show the one-line description and facility tag per option.
3. For each chosen feature, record a small manifest:
   - `slug` — kebab-case, drives `R/<slug>.R` and `test_<slug>.R`.
   - target function name(s) (with `()`),
   - one-line description + source pointer,
   - the ROADMAP bullet text (to delete from ROADMAP on success — mirror the prior session).

## Stage 2 — Implement each feature (linear, one at a time)

For each selected feature, in turn:

1. **Read 2-3 existing functions** in `R/` to match conventions before writing anything.
2. Write `R/<slug>.R`:
   - Base pipe `|>` (never `%>%`); `function() {}` not `\()`; `{}` around every if/else body.
   - Full roxygen2 (`@param`, `@return`, `@export`, `@examples`, `@author`), matching the
     style of neighbouring functions.
   - Guard every Suggests-package call with `requireNamespace("<pkg>", quietly = TRUE)` +
     `cli::cli_abort(...)`. **Note** any new Suggests dependency for Stage 3 — do **not**
     edit `DESCRIPTION` yet.
   - CRAN constraints: primary example in `\donttest{}`, variants in `\dontrun{}`; cap any
     per-sample loop at 5 samples via
     `prune_samples(sample_names(data_fungi_mini)[1:5], data_fungi_mini)`
     (`data_fungi_mini` has 137 samples); external-tool examples in `\dontrun{}`.
3. Write `tests/testthat/test_<slug>.R`:
   - **Underscore** filename — repo convention. (r_development.md says `test-<name>.R` with a
     hyphen; that's wrong for this repo. Use underscore.)
   - `skip_on_cran()` as the **first line** if the whole file should skip on CRAN.
   - Minimal, few comments. Guard Suggests-dependent tests with
     `skip_if_not_installed("<pkg>")`.
4. **Validate this feature before moving on:**
   `NOT_CRAN=true Rscript -e "devtools::load_all('.'); testthat::test_file('tests/testthat/test_<slug>.R')"`
   (never append `2>&1` to Rscript calls). Don't start the next feature with this one red.

Do **not** edit any global file in this stage — collect the per-feature notes (function
names, new Suggests deps, NEWS bullet text, `_pkgdown.yml` section, spelling terms) for
Stage 3.

## Stage 3 — Consolidate (ONE pass over the global files)

Once all features are implemented and individually green, do the global edits once, in order:

1. **NEWS.md** — add every bullet, then **re-sort the whole current-version list**
   alphabetically by function name (bullets without a function name go first). Present tense,
   function name in `backticks()` early in the bullet.
2. **_pkgdown.yml** — add each new topic to the section it belongs in.
3. **DESCRIPTION** — add all new Suggests deps in a single edit, keep the list sorted.
4. **inst/WORDLIST** — add all flagged terms; keep it `LC_ALL=C` sorted, en-US.
5. **`air format`** — run, then **scope the diff**: `air format .` reformats unrelated files.
   Revert incidental reformats so the diff stays feature-scoped
   (`git checkout -- <unrelated.R>`), or format only the changed files.
6. **`devtools::document()` ONCE** → regenerates `NAMESPACE` + `man/` with all new exports
   together.
7. **Carryover check** — `git diff NAMESPACE man/` and flag anything **not** attributable to
   the new features (e.g. a prior commit that never ran `document()`, like the
   `rarefy_even_depth_pq` carryover). Report it; don't silently bundle it.
8. **Spelling** — `Rscript -e "spelling::spell_check_package()"` once; fix real typos, add
   legitimate jargon to `inst/WORDLIST`.
9. **Full test suite** — once, `NOT_CRAN=true Rscript -e "devtools::test()"`. Swallow output;
   show `✓` if green, dump on failure.

## Stage 4 — One Quarto demo for all features

Write `<workspace>/arround_MiscMetabar/<name>_demo.qmd` covering **every** new feature in one
document:

- A setup chunk that `devtools::load_all()`s the dev package and loads
  `data(data_fungi_mini)`, capped to 5 samples for any per-sample work.
- One section per feature: a short prose explanation + a **runnable** chunk. Tool-dependent
  features (fastp etc.) use the dry-run / `cmd_is_run = FALSE` path or `is_*_installed()` so
  the chunk evaluates without the external binary.
- Close with `sessionInfo()`.
- **Verify the chunks actually evaluate** (render, or source each chunk) against the
  5-sample subset — a demo that only *looks* runnable is a regression waiting to happen.

## Stage 5 — ROADMAP cleanup & report

1. **ROADMAP.md** — delete the shipped bullets and any now-dead `(source:)` pointer rows. If a
   prototype in `en_cours_de_dev.qmd` is fully shipped, offer to remove that section too.
2. **Report** a table of features shipped (function · test · NEWS · pkgdown · man), the test
   result, spelling status, the demo path, and any `NAMESPACE`/`man/` **carryover** flagged
   in Stage 3.7. State plainly that the diff is left **uncommitted on `dev`** for the
   developer, and that **R CMD check was not run** — offer `/r-check`.
3. Open the html report in a browser.

## Important rules

- **Never commit** — the developer commits.
- **No global-file edits while implementing a feature.** All of NEWS / _pkgdown / DESCRIPTION
  / WORDLIST / NAMESPACE / man happen once, in Stage 3.
- **When scope or design is ambiguous, ask the developer** — especially before writing a
  custom implementation of something a CRAN package (vegan, etc.) likely already provides.
- Tests are `test_<slug>.R` (underscore). Never append `2>&1` to Rscript Bash calls.
- Prefer items with an existing `(source:)` prototype; they're the low-risk path.

---

# dbpq

- **[High / hard]** Add ACVPMBD (https://github.com/Andreas-Bio/ACVPMBD, https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/pdf/10.1002/edn3.70125) idea for plant-specific databases.

- **[High / moderate]** Add download functions for GTDB (bacteria + archaea), MIDORI2, and KSGP databases. See Box 1 in ISME Commun. 2025 for archaea-specific workflow documentation.
  (source: `ROADMAP.md`:43–45)

- **[Medium / moderate]** New vignette: measuring and visualising reference database usage via OpenAlex + `openalexR` (direct citations + database-name search).
  (source: `ROADMAP.md`:47)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Integrate selected `refdb` package functions: `igraph_from_taxo()`, `refdb_filter_seq_primer()`, `valid_taxo_rank()`, `refdb_sample_tax()`, `refdb_export_*()`.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)

---

# comparpq

- **[Medium / moderate]** Finalize distance-to-true-community metric in the in-silico benchmarking workflow.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/benchmar_assign_taxo/analysis/benchmark.qmd`:402)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Loop to create multiple communities at the last timestep of miaSim Hubbell model (multiple independent simulations for robustness).
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/benchmar_assign_taxo/In_silico_simulation.qmd`:69)

- **[Low / easy]** Optionally add "fake" taxa to benchmark for TRUE-negative / trade-off analysis. Not urgent.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/benchmar_assign_taxo/R/functions.R`:103)

---

# taxinfo

## Dataset bundling

- **[High / moderate]** Bundle FungalTraits database as `inst/extdata`. **License check required before inclusion.**
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/ARCHIVES/mitis_taxonomic_project.qmd`:1471)

- **[High / moderate]** Bundle TAXREFv18 as `inst/extdata`. **License check required before inclusion.**
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/ARCHIVES/mitis_taxonomic_project.qmd`:1490)

content.text:
This is a poor fit for the feature-batch workflow as-is, and I want your call before doing anything. Three concrete problems:

1. **Dirty tree**: dbpq's `dev` has ~2000 lines of uncommitted in-progress work (29 files) that already implements `download_midori2_db`, `download_ksgp_db`, `download_ltplus_db` — overlapping the main `[High/moderate]` "GTDB/MIDORI2/KSGP downloads" ROADMAP item. The batch workflow assumes a clean tree so it can produce a scoped diff and detect `document()` carryover; neither is possible on top of this.
2. **No easy items**: dbpq's ROADMAP has only 4 items, all Medium/moderate or High/hard — none match the skill's `High/easy`+`Medium/easy` default.
3. **Convention mismatches**: dbpq uses **hyphen** test files (`test-download.R`), and it's a FASTA-database package with **no `data_fungi_mini`/phyloseq** objects, so the skill's CRAN/demo scaffolding doesn't apply.

Let me get your direction:

questions.question: dbpq's dev tree has ~2000 lines of uncommitted in-progress work (already implementing MIDORI2/KSGP/LTPlus downloads). How should I proceed?

options.label: Stash/commit first (Recommended)
options.description: You commit or stash the current in-progress diff, then I start clean on dev so the batch produces a scoped diff and a meaningful carryover check. Tell me once done.

options.label: Build on top of current diff
options.description: I add features into the existing uncommitted work. Accept that the consolidation pass (document/NAMESPACE/man, NEWS sort) will intermix with your existing changes and the carryover check won't be clean.

options.label: Hold off for now
options.description: Don't run the batch. You finish the current dbpq development first; re-run /r-feature-batch dbpq later on a clean tree.

questions.question: dbpq has no easy ROADMAP items. If we proceed, which direction (all are Medium/moderate+)?

options.label: refdb integration
options.description: [Medium/moderate] Wrap selected refdb functions: igraph_from_taxo(), refdb_filter_seq_primer(), valid_taxo_rank(), refdb_sample_tax(), refdb_export_*(). New Suggests: refdb.

options.label: GTDB download
options.description: The missing piece of the download item: download_gtdb_db() (bacteria+archaea). MIDORI2/KSGP/LTPlus already exist in the uncommitted diff; GTDB does not.

options.label: OpenAlex usage vignette
options.description: [Medium/moderate] New vignette measuring/visualising reference-database usage via OpenAlex + openalexR (citations + database-name search).

options.label: Decide after tree question
options.description: Hold feature selection until the tree-state decision is made.
